text:
In a small, sleepy town nestled among whispering forests and where the sun never really set, lived a girl named Lyra. With curls as wild as the enchanted woods and a spirit as restless as the wind, Lyra had a secret: she loved the stars.

Every night, under the cloak of darkness, Lyra would slip out of her house and make her way to the highest point she could find. There, she would gaze at the sky, her heart syncing with the distant whispers of distant stars. To her, the stars weren’t just lights in the night sky; they were distant friends, guiding and reassuring her in ways she couldn’t fully understand.

One evening, as Lyra’s gaze drifted towards a particularly bright and familiar constellation, she felt a sudden, inexplicable shift in her reality. It was as though reality had twisted just a bit, revealing the existence of beings akin to constellations herself used to gaze upon.

A voice, neither quite earthly, yet resonant
